name: Pallas
description: Pallas, in Roman mythology, is a figure shrouded in some ambiguity and often conflated or associated with other figures, particularly in connection to the story of Aeneas and the founding of Rome. He is best known as the son of Evander, the Arcadian king who settled on the Palatine Hill before the arrival of Aeneas. Pallas was a valiant warrior and a close companion of Aeneas, representing the noble qualities of youth and courage. His tragic death in battle against Turnus, the Rutulian king and Aeneas's rival for Lavinia's hand, is a pivotal moment in Virgil's Aeneid. Evander entrusts Pallas to Aeneas's care, hoping that the experience will shape him into a wise and just ruler. Aeneas takes on a paternal role, guiding and protecting Pallas. During the war between the Trojans and the Rutulians, Pallas displays exceptional bravery, but ultimately falls victim to Turnus's superior strength. Turnus, in a moment of brutal victory, strips Pallas of his belt and wears it as a trophy. This act of desecration enrages Aeneas and fuels his desire for revenge, ultimately leading to the final duel between Aeneas and Turnus and the establishment of Roman power. Pallas's death serves as a symbol of the sacrifices made in the founding of Rome and the heavy burden of leadership. He is remembered as a youthful hero whose potential was tragically cut short, and his memory inspires Aeneas to fulfill his destiny and create a lasting legacy. The grief of Evander, Pallas's father, deeply resonates with the human cost of war and the fragility of life. Pallas's story highlights the themes of duty, honor, and the price of empire in Roman mythology.
subtitle: Valiant son of Evander, fallen hero

keyMyths.title: Pallas's Death
keyMyths.description: Pallas is killed by Turnus in battle, a pivotal moment in the Aeneid. Turnus takes Pallas's belt as a trophy, enraging Aeneas and fueling his desire for revenge.

keyMyths.title: Entrustment to Aeneas
keyMyths.description: Evander entrusts his son Pallas to Aeneas's care, hoping he will learn from the Trojan hero. Aeneas acts as a mentor and protector to the young Pallas.

keyMyths.title: Alliance with Aeneas
keyMyths.description: Pallas and his father Evander ally with Aeneas upon his arrival in Italy. They provide support and warriors to aid Aeneas in his fight against Turnus.

sources.author: Virgil
sources.description: The primary source for the story of Pallas and his role in the Trojan War in Italy.
sources.text: The primary source for the story of Pallas and his role in the Trojan War in Italy.

sources.author: Robert Graves
sources.description: Provides context and analysis of Pallas's role within broader mythological themes.
